finding_results.notes: Broke the prompt-answer-explanation ladder at seven points across the Spike-Hector exchange. Non-clean replies from Spike at three points (lines 223, 289, 365) where he deflects physically or gives non-advancing responses instead of asking the next neat question. Hector self-corrects, trails off, or searches for wording in six turns (lines 225, 247, 291, 297, 349/353, 435). Removed the snap 'Useful' / 'You want useful, go ask a ledger' volley (former lines 305-307) that exemplified the clipped-question/clipped-answer/polished-corrective pattern. Removed two instances of narrator-admires-dialogue commentary ('That landed' at former line 189; 'The line landed closer to the truth than Spike liked' at former line 383). Preserved the conversation's information delivery, emotional arc, and all continuity payloads while roughening its surface into messier, more spoken texture.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 8 now keeps Spike inside the Ayane disclosure, clarifies the route evidence he failed to read at the time, and lands the closing moral turn through concrete freight memory instead of abstract explanation.

Removed the 'Useful' / 'You want useful' snap exchange and two narrator-admires-dialogue instances ('landed'). The conversation now advances through messier, more realistic pressure cadence while preserving its emotional shape, information payload, and all continuity anchors.
